transcription.full_text: Hello, I just want to quickly walk through where I'm at with my designs for home care agreements and the capturing of the signature. So I'll just quickly walk through what I've got so far just to get some feedback. So prior to anything showing in portal, the sales team will convert the lead to consumer, send out the push form and then send out the sample agreement. When that sample agreement gets sent, ideally that's when you'll be able to see the sample agreement as part of the client's package too. So you'll be able to log into the portal, see this new portal and package architecture. Under profile, you'll have the agreement and fees tabs alongside client details and care circle. And within that, you'd see your home care agreement and all other agreements as well. Ideally, the variations of any of those agreements will be sat in the relevant category rather than a kind of section on its own. So here I'm just showing where you'd have the home care agreement. It'll outline the parties that are involved. Obviously for home care agreement, it's trilogy care, but for other agreements would be all your suppliers. You've got the created date, the statuses, which would change between sample pending, which would be pending signature and signed. And then you'll have the signed date and then the valid until once that has been completed. The filter search and filter by those different statuses as well, just for if you've got lots of agreements on the package. So you'd be able to go click view, you'd see the sample agreement and you'd go and convert it with the watermark and you'd be able to go convert it to sign when you're ready to capture that signature. So whether that's in the meeting or perhaps it's after the meeting, it will have the status of sample. And in terms of actually just viewing the document, in terms of interactivity, you can zoom in, zoom out, click between pages or just scroll and then expand to view full page. But once you convert to sign, then essentially all it does is just opens up the section on the right hand side, which captures the main contents of the agreement and then also the signature collection method. So if you're in the meeting and you want to capture a verbal signature, you'd go and select verbal signature and it will display these relevant fields afterwards. So the signed date, so that's the meeting date, the call ID and the full recording URL for from air call. And then the consent, consent transcript would just be like a copied and pasted section of the transcript, which actually contains the verbal consent. Not sure if we feel like that's necessary or not, but I've added it in and then just who it's been captured by. Similar for the signed document, you just have the signed date, upload the document and then again, who has been uploaded by and you'd be able to go and submit the two. And then it would just confirm that it's been signed and then it would navigate you to a completed state with the table view again. If you go and select a digital signature from client, then what it will do is it will remove the submit button and then it will display this card where you can go and click the button to say send email to client. And that will trigger an email which provides a link to portal for the client for them to go and sign digitally there. Once that's, once that has been sent, this card would update to a pending state, which is just saying that the signature request has been sent, who it's been sent by, what time, and then just that it's awaiting the signature. You can then go and resend that link as well if they claim that they haven't received it. I'll go through the client slide in a second. I'll just quickly walk through the different states. So for the signed document, this is what the completed state looks like. You'd then be able to see a full overview, you'd be able to download the document, see who was uploaded by, etc. and then terminate if necessary. And then you'd be able to flick between history as well, just similar to what we do with bills, etc. And you'd see like when a sample was created, when it was sent, when it was signed, and then ideally any variations of the agreement as well afterwards would all be contained here. And then again, pretty much the same for all of the different types of consent. So the verbal signature would have the URL to the recording, the consent transcript captured here as well, and then again, the history. And then for the client signature, just kind of capturing what they've checked off, and the date at which that was completed. So if you click terminate package, I've just got the the days due in one day, and they have different colours of just informational for 30 days, warning for seven, and then red, red display for high attention. Then we've got the on the valid until it will just display the number of days until that document expires to. And then as you can see here as well, you can see all the different statuses. Okay, I'll quickly walk over to the client side. So as soon as you've sent that request for a digital signature, the client would receive an email, I haven't got the email here, but it's just to show that you'd have an email there, you'd be able to click a link, and then that would take you and the client into portals, they'd log in, and it will direct them straight to the relevant section. So in this case, the home care agreement, and they'd be able to sign on the right hand side. So it will say it'll say who you're signing as or who you're logged in as. And then just outlining what this means by selecting that checkbox, and then that you can send to signing it electronically. So as soon as that checkbox is clicked, you'd be able to sign agreement, and then that would just convert to a signed document. I've enabled the decline button, I'm not sure if this is something that we necessarily want. But again, if they don't agree with anything, then they can decline. And then that will trigger us to go and follow up rather than just not signing and us not knowing at what point we should be following up. But as soon as you sign, it will display a modal again, similar to the internal view, and it will just navigate them back to the table again, wherever they were, I've just created a mobile view of this as well. Obviously, this will be available in the native app. So essentially, this is what it would look like, you'd go and click view your agreements, and it will display a preview, which you'd be able to scroll, you'd have to exit it and then sign below just so it's not super cluttered on the page. And then this is what the broader table view would look like as well. So you'd have your agreement, you'd navigate off to that, and then you'd see all of the individual agreements listed below like this. So in this case, where let's say a client logs in, and they didn't receive the link for the email, they just choose to log in first, then it will take them here to the dashboard, they receive an alert on their main dashboard, which would say please sign your home care agreement, and that would navigate them straight to here, like we had before. Or if they happen to click through to their agreements and fees, which they'd be able to go click drop down their name, select agreement and fees, and then that'll take them to this table view page. And again, similar to the internal view, they'd be able to go and click that view button on the table, and it will navigate them to this to the to this agreement, or if it is not official agreement at this point, and they'd be navigated to the sample agreement. And again, they can't do anything other than download, but it just allows them to preview the document where required. So that's everything that I've got so far. Yeah, just keen to get some feedback earlier on and if there's any fields that are required for home care agreements, and also any of the other agreements as well. good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good sex and good Thank you.
